marketClose.sessionSummary: 943 trades across 369,700 shares. Turnover ~K10.85 million. LASI flat at 25,689.97. Breadth negative: 1 advancer, 3 decliners, 16 unchanged. DCZM led decliners (-0.08% to K24.60). Trading in 20 of 24 listed securities.

analysis.marketConditionDetail: The LASI closed unchanged at 25,689.97 for the second consecutive session. Market cap rose slightly to K335.48 billion (K145.4 billion excluding Shoprite). Negative breadth with 1 advancer vs 3 decliners. Activity concentrated in ATEL, ZSUG, and BATA. DCZM continued its slide (-0.08%). Low turnover of K10.85 million across 369,700 shares — a sleepy Monday session typical of pre-election caution.

observations.title: DCZM — Continued Decline, Now 3 Sessions of Losses
observations.detail: Dot Com Zambia fell another 0.08% to K24.60, marking its third consecutive declining session. At K24.60 with PE 36.42x and 2.93% gross margins, the stock remains priced on narrative, not fundamentals. FY2025 revenue of K665.6M yielded only K6.75M net profit (1.01% margin). eFuel and Smart Cards segments near-zero revenue. Caution warranted.

observations.title: ATEL — Still the Liquidity Anchor
observations.detail: Airtel Zambia accounted for ~34% of total value traded at K3.74M. At K169.87 with PE 10.02x and 7.06% dividend yield (K11.99 DPS), it remains LuSE's most liquid and institutionally-owned counter. FY2025 revenue K8.95B, net income K1.76B. Ex-div date was May 6. Strong fundamentals but beta -0.18 suggests low correlation with broader moves.

observations.title: ZNCO — Deep Value Banking at PE 6.87x
observations.detail: Zanaco closed at K9.57 (stockanalysis.com) / K9.62 (LuSE sheet). PE 6.87x, dividend yield 4.58%, 7 consecutive years of dividend growth. Revenue K6.35B, net income K2.01B (31.65% margin). ROE 31.58%. The discount to intrinsic value remains compelling in a post-restructuring Zambia.

observations.title: ZSUG — K69.09 on Thin Float, 2026 Headwinds
observations.detail: Zambia Sugar MD Oswald Magwenzi noted 2026 is 'more challenging' than prior year. At K69.09 with PE 19.58x and 1.59% dividend yield, valuation looks stretched. 75% held by ABF leaves negligible free float. FCF negative at -K111.9M. Great company, terrible trading stock.

observations.title: ZCCM-IH — Strategic Moves Accelerating
observations.detail: At K153.07 with PE 6.5x, ZCCM-IH is the cheapest mining play on LuSE. Recent developments: (1) 600MW coal plant deal with Wonderful Group announced Jun 1, (2) Constitutional Court judgment clearing Mopani dispute, (3) Strategic review of Mopani stake ongoing. Copper above $10,000/tonne supports thesis.

riskFlags: K190B of K335.48B market cap is Shoprite Holdings — only K145.4B in domestic Zambian equities
4 stocks recorded ZERO shares traded (SHOP, FARM, REIZUSD, and potentially INDO/PRIM/ENRG)
